hook.text: Some orchids offer no reward at all—they trick pollinators into visiting with false promises.

buildup.text: Bee orchids mimic the shape and scent of female bees so convincingly that males try to mate.

discovery.text: The deceived male picks up pollen and carries it to the next fake female—mission accomplished.

twist.text: About one-third of orchid species are deceptive—they never produce nectar or any real reward.

climax.text: Deception works because pollinators can't always tell real from fake on a quick visit.

quiz.question: How do bee orchids attract pollinators?
options: They mimic the shape and scent of female bees
